ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for sausage-and-egg English muffin burger with hashbrown; 'breakfast sandwich' (FOODON_00002383) is the closest defensible match.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for this specific breakfast burger set meal; A0CKP (burger/sandwich, meat-based composite) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps precisely to this composite fast-food breakfast item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 280 g, comprising one assembled English muffin breakfast burger (sausage patty, fried egg, sliced cheese, two muffin halves) plus one hashbrown patty, as typically served in Singapore fast-food and café outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Sausage and Egg Breakfast Burger with Hashbrown — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Sausage and Egg Breakfast Burger with Hashbrown' (cr_id F08020045). All per-100g values are as reported; confidence 0.95 per source metadata.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; English muffin GI reference (GI ~77) and composite meal adjustment for protein/fat dampening
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific composite meal. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on: (1) English muffin GI ~77 (University of Sydney GI Database, white English muffin); (2) hashbrown/fried potato GI ~75 (University of Sydney GI Database); (3) composite meal GI dampening effect of protein (10.81g/100g) and fat (13.39g/100g) components, which typically reduce effective GI by 10–15 units in mixed meals (per Brand-Miller et al., AJCN). Weighted blend of carbohydrate-contributing components (muffin ~55%, hashbrown ~30%, residual ~15%) yields estimated composite GI of 65. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI 65 × carbohydrate per serving (19.47g/100g × 280g serving = 54.5g carbohydrate) / 100 = 35.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 556 mg/100g with very low potassium offset (114 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: At 556 mg sodium per 100g and only 114 mg potassium per 100g, a single 280g serving delivers approximately 1,557 mg sodium — exceeding 67% of the 2,300 mg daily limit — with negligible potassium offset; individuals with hypertension should avoid this meal or request no added salt and reduced condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Estimated medium-to-high GI (~65) from refined English muffin and deep-fried hashbrown with low dietary fibre (2.03g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of a refined English muffin (GI ~77) and deep-fried hashbrown (GI ~75) produces an estimated composite GI of 65 with only 2.03g dietary fibre per 100g, resulting in a high glycemic load per serving (~35g); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, omit the hashbrown, and choose a wholegrain alternative where possible.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.8g/100g), trans fat (0.2g/100g), high sodium (556mg/100g), and processed meat components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal contains saturated fat (~4.8g/100g), trans fat (0.2g/100g from processed sausage and deep-frying), and 556mg sodium per 100g — all established cardiovascular risk factors; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal or substitute with a grilled lean protein on a wholegrain base with no hashbrown.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried hashbrown (AGE formation), processed sausage (saturated fat, sodium nitrates, arachidonic acid), trans fat 0.2g/100g, nightshade potato
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The deep-fried hashbrown generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that exacerbate joint inflammation, the processed sausage contributes sodium nitrates and arachidonic acid, and trans fat at 0.2g/100g is pro-inflammatory at any amount; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this meal entirely and substitute with grilled lean protein and non-nightshade vegetables.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Sausage and Egg Breakfast Burger with Hashbrown is a Western-style fast-food breakfast set meal popular in Singapore cafés, fast-food chains, and school canteens. It consists of a grilled pork sausage patty and a fried egg layered with a slice of processed cheese, sandwiched between two halves of a toasted English muffin, served alongside a deep-fried shredded potato hashbrown patty. The English muffin provides a slightly chewy, lightly crisp exterior with a soft interior; the sausage patty is savoury and moderately fatty; the fried egg adds richness; and the hashbrown contributes a crispy, starchy contrast. Nutritionally, per 100g the meal delivers 262 kcal, 10.81g protein, 13.39g fat (including approximately 4.8g saturated fat and 0.2g trans fat), 19.47g carbohydrate, 2.03g dietary fibre, and 556mg sodium. The high sodium content (556mg/100g) and low potassium (114mg/100g) make this meal a concern for individuals with hypertension. The refined carbohydrate base and deep-fried hashbrown produce an estimated composite GI of 65 (medium-high), with a high glycemic load per serving, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat, trans fat, and processed meat components raise cardiovascular disease risk. The deep-fried potato hashbrown generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), making this meal particularly unsuitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. This meal is best consumed occasionally and in reduced portions, with modifications such as omitting the hashbrown and choosing wholegrain bread where available.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08020045) with confidence 0.95 per source metadata. The food is an exact match in the primary regional approved source. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence medium for GI component only) based on University of Sydney GI Database reference values for English muffin and fried potato, with documented composite meal dampening methodology.
notes: 1. Saturated fat estimated at ~4.8g/100g based on typical pork sausage and processed cheese composition; HPB source does not separately report saturated fat for this entry — value derived from USDA FoodData Central component analogues (pork breakfast sausage patty, processed cheese slice, English muffin, hashbrown) weighted by approximate ingredient proportions. 2. Trans fat estimated at 0.2g/100g based on processed sausage and deep-frying contribution; not explicitly reported in HPB source. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite meal are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for F08020045, and no sufficiently close composite analogue exists in USDA FoodData Central to provide reliable per-100g estimates for these fields. 4. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific composite meal was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16. 5. Glycemic load per serving (280g): GL = 65 × (19.47 × 2.80) / 100 = 65 × 54.52 / 100 = 35.4. The field glycemic_load_per_serving is set to 35.4 reflecting the standard 280g serving. 6. Halal status is not confirmed — pork sausage is a primary ingredient; this meal is not halal. 7. Serving size of 280g is an estimate based on typical Singapore fast-food breakfast set meal weights; no standardised serving weight is specified in the HPB source.
